subject: Chemistry
q:
Resonance in carbonate ion CO32- is

Which of the following is true?
question:
Resonance in carbonate ion CO32- is

Which of the following is true?
options: It is possible to identify each structure individually by some physical or chemical method.
All these structures are in dynamic equilibrium with each other.
Each structure exists for equal amount of time.

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msubsup><mi>CO</mi><mn>3</mn><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msubsup></math> has a single structure i.e., resonance hybrid of the above three structures.
solution:
Resonating structure are hypothetical and resonance hybrid is real structure which is weighted average of all the resonating structures.

So, CO32- has a single structure i.e. resonance hybrid of the three structures.

subject: Chemistry
q: The number of hyperconjugation structures involved to stabilize carbocation formed in the above reaction is __________.
question: The number of hyperconjugation structures involved to stabilize carbocation formed in the above reaction is __________.
solution:
In the first step of the reaction, protonation of alcohol takes place and carbocation is formed after removal of water. The carbocation formed undergo rearrangement to get stability. In the given, methyl shift takes place to convert secondary carbocation to tertiary carbocation. The alpha hydrogen to the positive charged carbon are involved in hyperconjugation.

So, number of hyperconjugation structures in most stable carbocation

= 6 + 1 = 7

subject: Chemistry
q:
In Dumas' method for estimation of nitrogen 0.4 g of an organic compound gave 60 mL of nitrogen collected at 300 K temperature and 715 mm Hg pressure. The percentage composition of nitrogen in the compound is
(Given : Aqueous tension at $300 \mathrm{~K}=15 \mathrm{~mm} \mathrm{Hg}$)
question:
In Dumas' method for estimation of nitrogen 0.4 g of an organic compound gave 60 mL of nitrogen collected at 300 K temperature and 715 mm Hg pressure. The percentage composition of nitrogen in the compound is
(Given : Aqueous tension at $300 \mathrm{~K}=15 \mathrm{~mm} \mathrm{Hg}$)
solution:
$\text { Pressure of } \begin{aligned}
\mathrm{N}_2 \text { gas evolved } & =715-15 \\
& =700 \mathrm{~mm} \mathrm{Hg} \\
& =\frac{700}{760} \mathrm{~atm}.
\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned}
\therefore \text { Mole of } \mathrm{N}_2 \text { evolved } & =\frac{\mathrm{PV}}{\mathrm{RT}} \\
& =\frac{700 \times 60 \times 10^{-3}}{760 \times 0.0821 \times 300} \\
& =0.0022 \mathrm{~mole}
\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \mathrm{wt}. \%$ of nitrogen in compound
$\begin{aligned}
& =\frac{\text { wt. of nitrogen }}{\text { wt. of compound }} \times 100 \\
& =\frac{0.063}{0.4} \times 100 \\
& =15.71 \%
\end{aligned}$

subject: Chemistry
q: Which among the following purification methods is based on the principle of “Solubility” in two different solvents?
question: Which among the following purification methods is based on the principle of “Solubility” in two different solvents?
solution:
In chemistry, differential extraction is defined as a technique that is used to separate any organic compound that is present in an aqueous solution from the rest of the solution. The process of liquid-liquid extraction is the most commonly used in the chemistry laboratory, and it takes place in a separatory funnel.

The organic compound is later recovered by the process of distillation or evaporation. The process of continuous extraction is used in cases when the solubility of the compound is less in the organic solvent.

Length of the column or <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>TLC</mi></math> Plate
Physical state of the pure compound
solution: In chromatography technique, the purification of a compound is independent of the physical state of the pure compound.

subject: Chemistry
q: The IUPAC name of ethylidene chloride is
question: The IUPAC name of ethylidene chloride is
solution:
1,1-dichloroethane

Ethylidene chloride is a geminal dihalide with two carbon atoms. In common name system, gem-dihalides are named as alkylidene halides and vic-dihalides are named as alkylene dihalides. The structure of ethylidene chloride is: 

CH3−CHCl2.

question: Compound(s) which will liberate carbon dioxide with sodium bicarbonate solution is/are :
solution:
Compounds which are more acidic then H2CO3, gives CO2 gas on reaction with NaHCO3. Compound B i.e. Benzoic acid and compound C i.e. picric acid both are more acidic than H2CO3.

equilibrium favours forward direction and CO2↑ is liberated.

Equilibrium favours backward direction and CO2↑ is not liberated.

subject: Chemistry
q: Appearance of blood red colour, on treatment of the sodium fusion extract of an organic compound with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>FeSO</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math> in presence of concentrated <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi>SO</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math> indicates the presence of element/s
question: Appearance of blood red colour, on treatment of the sodium fusion extract of an organic compound with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>FeSO</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math> in presence of concentrated <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi>SO</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math> indicates the presence of element/s
solution:
The sodium fusion test, or Lassaigne's test, is used in elemental analysis for the qualitative determination of the presence of foreign elements, namely halogens, nitrogen, and sulphur, in an organic compound.

In Lassaigne's test, if both N and S are present in the organic compound, they are converted to NaSCN.

If SCN- is added to a solution with iron(III) ions, a blood-red solution forms mainly due to the formation of [Fe(SCN)(H2O)5]2+

q:
Given below are two statements :
Statement I : Aniline is less basic than acetamide.
Statement II : In aniline, the lone pair of electrons on nitrogen atom is delocalised over benzene ring due to resonance and hence less available to a proton.
Choose the most appropriate option ;
question:
Given below are two statements :
Statement I : Aniline is less basic than acetamide.
Statement II : In aniline, the lone pair of electrons on nitrogen atom is delocalised over benzene ring due to resonance and hence less available to a proton.
Choose the most appropriate option ;
options: Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></math> is true but statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>II</mi></math> is false.
Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></math> is false but statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>II</mi></math> is true.
Both statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></math> and statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>II</mi></math> are true.
Both statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></math> and statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>II</mi></math> are false.
solution:
Explanation :- aniline is more basic than acetamide because in acetamide, lone pair of nitrogen is delocalised to more electronegative element oxygen.

In Aniline lone pair of nitrogen delocalised over benzene ring.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Chromatographic technique/s based on the principle of differential adsorption is/are
A. Column chromatography B. Thin layer chromatography C. Paper chromatography

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:
question:
Chromatographic technique/s based on the principle of differential adsorption is/are
A. Column chromatography B. Thin layer chromatography C. Paper chromatography

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:
solution:
Adsorption chromatography is defined as a type of chromatography in which separation occurs based on adsorption. Partition chromatography is a type of chromatography in which separation is based on partition. Adsorption chromatography is a liquid-solid extraction. Partition chromatography is a liquid-liquid extraction.

Column chromatography and Thin layer chromatography are examples of adsorption chromatography. Paper chromatography is an example of partition chromatography.

subject: Chemistry
q:
In the estimation of bromine, 0.5 g of an organic compound gave 0.40 g of silver bromide. The percentage of bromine in the given compound is % (nearest integer)
(Relative atomic masses of Ag and Br are 108u and 80u, respectively).
question:
In the estimation of bromine, 0.5 g of an organic compound gave 0.40 g of silver bromide. The percentage of bromine in the given compound is % (nearest integer)
(Relative atomic masses of Ag and Br are 108u and 80u, respectively).
solution:
The estimation of halogens in organic compounds is usually done by carius method.

Percentage of Bromine =Atomic mass of BrMolecular mass of AgBr×Mass of AgBr×100Mass of the organic compound

=80×0.4×100188×0.5

=34%

subject: Chemistry
q: The following structures are
question: The following structures are
solution:
The given structures have a central carbon atom bonded to four groups: $-\text{Br}$, $-\text{Cl}$, $-\text{CH}_3$, and $-\text{Me}$.

Since $-\text{CH}_3$ and $-\text{Me}$ both represent the same alkyl group (methyl), the central carbon atom is bonded to two identical groups.

For a carbon atom to be a chiral center, it must be bonded to four different groups. Because this condition is not met, the central carbon is not a stereocenter and the molecule is achiral.

Both structures represent the same achiral compound (2-bromo-2-chloropropane) drawn in slightly different orientations. Therefore, they are identical molecules.

Answer: identical molecules.

subject: Chemistry
q:
In the sulphur estimation, 0.20 g of a pure organic compound gave 0.40 g of barium sulphate. The percentage of sulphur in the compound is ______ $\times 10^{-1} \%$.
(Molar mass : $\mathrm{O}=16, \mathrm{~S}=32, \mathrm{Ba}=137$ in $\mathrm{g} \mathrm{mol}^{-1}$ )
question:
In the sulphur estimation, 0.20 g of a pure organic compound gave 0.40 g of barium sulphate. The percentage of sulphur in the compound is ______ $\times 10^{-1} \%$.
(Molar mass : $\mathrm{O}=16, \mathrm{~S}=32, \mathrm{Ba}=137$ in $\mathrm{g} \mathrm{mol}^{-1}$ )
solution:
$\begin{array}{ll}\text { Organic Compound } \longrightarrow & \mathrm{BaSO}_4 \\ 0.20 \mathrm{gm} & 0.40 \mathrm{gm} \\ & \frac{0.40}{233} \mathrm{~mol}\left(\mathrm{BaSO}_4\right) \\ & \frac{0.40}{233} \mathrm{~mol} \text { (Sulphur) } \\ & \frac{0.40}{233} \times 32 \mathrm{gm} \text { (sulphur) }\end{array}$



$\% \mathrm{~S}=\frac{\frac{0.40 \times 32}{233} \times 100}{0.20}=27.5 \%$ or $275 \times 10^{-1} \%$

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two statements : one is labelled as Assertion (A) and the other is labelled as
Assertion (A): A simple distillation can be used to separate a mixture of propanol and propanone.
Reason (R): Two liquids with a difference of more than 20°C in their boiling points can be separated by simple distillations.
In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
question:
Given below are two statements : one is labelled as Assertion (A) and the other is labelled as
Assertion (A): A simple distillation can be used to separate a mixture of propanol and propanone.
Reason (R): Two liquids with a difference of more than 20°C in their boiling points can be separated by simple distillations.
In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
options: Both (A) and (R) are correct but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).
Both (A) and (R) are correct and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
solution: Simple distilation is the method of separating mixtures based on differences in their volatilities in a boiling liquid mixture. The components in a sample mixture are vaporised by the application of heat and then immediately cooled by the action of cold water in a condenser. Two liquids with a difference of more than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> in their boiling points can be separated by simple distillations.

if molar mass of compound is M
